title: WpfHexEditor Class Diagram Visual State
description: Schema for .whcd twin files that persist the visual state of class diagrams next to their source file(s). Version 2 adds swimlanes, snap, view-mode, collapsed sections, and custom node heights.

version.description: Schema version. 1 = layout only. 2 = layout + view-mode + swimlanes + snap + collapsed sections + custom heights.

sourceFiles.description: Source file path(s) this diagram was generated from. May be a single .cs file or an entire folder.

offsetX.description: Horizontal pan offset of the canvas viewport in logical pixels.

offsetY.description: Vertical pan offset of the canvas viewport in logical pixels.

minimapVisible.description: Whether the minimap overlay is visible.

minimapCorner.description: Corner of the canvas where the minimap is docked.

selectedNodeId.description: ID of the node selected when the diagram was last saved. Null if no selection.

showSwimLanes.description: Whether the namespace swimlane background layer is visible.

snapToGrid.description: Whether nodes snap to a 20 px grid during drag.

splitLayout.description: Split direction when viewMode is Split.

splitColRatio.description: Fraction [0.1–0.9] of horizontal space allocated to the code pane in horizontal splits.

splitRowRatio.description: Fraction [0.1–0.9] of vertical space allocated to the code pane in vertical splits.

collapsedSections.description: Flat list of collapsed member-section entries in the form 'nodeId:SectionName'. SectionName is one of: Fields, Properties, Methods, Events.

nodes.description: Per-node canvas positions, indexed by stable node ID.

id.description: Stable identifier for the node (survives source edits; defaults to the type name).

x.description: Horizontal canvas coordinate of the node's top-left corner in logical pixels.

y.description: Vertical canvas coordinate of the node's top-left corner in logical pixels.

width.description: Node card width in logical pixels.

height.description: Custom node card height in logical pixels set by the resize gripper. 0 means auto (no override).
